violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bar area was lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (butter, sauce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (plates) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Fruit salad and yogurt was observed held at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Stuffing dated (7/31) was observed past date of disposition. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit in the coffee area. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Working containers of sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Inspector observed condensate from the AC unit dripping onto the coffee machine. PIC cleaned and sanitized machine at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the front kitchen area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Refrigerator located in the coffee area was not in good repair, ambient observed at 45F. PIC took unit out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The wash sink on the 3-bay sink was in poor repair; inspector observed leak. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The wall in the ware washing area has an accumulation of black and brown debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen were lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

A pitcher and container were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation in the walk-in cooler. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
Menus are lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The hand-washing sink in the 3-bay area and bar were lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
A strainer was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Shellstock (mussels, clams, and oysters) located in the walk-in cooler and low boy unit did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. The person in charge placed the tag in the containers at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Shellfish tags (mussels, clams, and oysters) were not maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag was not noted. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Raw animal foods (such as raw lamb) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cheese) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as cheese during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Raw chicken was observed stored over raw seafood in the walk-in cooler. Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Raw eggs were observed held at 53 degrees Fahrenheit out of refrigeration. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, eggs had been out of refrigeration for about 2 hours. Eggs were relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool. Eggs must be refrigerated at all times. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Cooked onions were observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the prep unit for 2 hours. Cooked onions were relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool. Cooked onions were observed double panned. Establishment must discontinue double panning food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Food was observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit in low boy #5. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool. The ambient air temperature was observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. The low boy unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Low boy unit must be repaired/adjusted/replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Food is served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by flies in the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment must schedule pest control services. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen was observed ajar.Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by keeping the doors closed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Vegetables were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and flour was observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

A container of artichokes was observed stored in a container of mussels. The bottom of the artichoke container was observed touching the mussels. Containers must have a barrier between each other to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
Food was observed stored uncovered. Food must be covered to prevent contamination from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The bread warmer spatula was stored on an unclean surface (between bread warmer and wall) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Single-service articles (disposable cutlery) were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen and bar. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
